zh.lifeSummary: 當Nicholas de Singleton於1312年出生在英國藍卡爾吉可哈姆時，他的父親Gilbert de Singleton38歲，母親Isabelle De Singleton38歲。他和Margaret至少有2個兒子。他去世於1397年之後。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Nicholas de Singleton于1312年出生在英国藍卡爾吉可哈姆时，他的父亲Gilbert de Singleton38岁，母亲Isabelle De Singleton38岁。他和Margaret至少有2个儿子。他去世于1397年之后。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Singleton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Singleton (Lancashire), probably from Old English scingol ‘shingle, wooden roof tile’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Bearers of this surname are said to be descended from Ughtred de Sinleton, holder of lands in Amounderness wapentake (Lancashire) c. 1160–90. This name was first recorded in Ireland in 1308 and firmly established in Louth and Monaghan during the 17th century.
habitational name from Singleton (Sussex), probably from Old English sængel, sengel ‘bundle, tuft’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Shingleton
meanings: English: late medieval variant of   Singleton  .

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Nicholas de Singleton was born in 1312, in Kirkham, Lancashire, England, his father, Gilbert de Singleton, was 38 and his mother, Isabelle De Singleton, was 38. He had at least 2 sons with Margaret. He died after 1397.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Nicholas de Singleton est né en 1312, à Kirkham, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Gilbert de Singleton, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Isabelle De Singleton, avait 38 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Margaret. Il est décédé après 1397.

de.lifeSummary: Als Nicholas de Singleton 1312 in Kirkham, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gilbert de Singleton 38 und seine Mutter Isabelle De Singleton 38. Er hatte mit Margaret mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb nach 1397.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas de Singleton è nato il nel 1312 nel Kirkham, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Gilbert de Singleton, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Isabelle De Singleton, aveva 38 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Margaret. Morì dopo il 1397.

ja.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Singletonが1312年にイギリスランカシュアイアキルクハムで生まれた時，父Gilbert de Singletonは38歳，母Isabelle De Singletonは38歳であった。彼はMargaretとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼は1397年以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Nicholas de Singleton(이)가 1312년 영국 랭카실르 커카에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gilbert de Singleton(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabelle De Singleton(은)는 38세였다. 그는 Margaret(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1397년 이후 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Nicholas de Singleton nasceu em 1312, em Kirkham, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Gilbert de Singleton, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Isabelle De Singleton, tinha 38 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Margaret. Ele faleceu após 1397.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Nicholas de Singleton родился в 1312 г. в местности кирхам, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Gilbert de Singleton было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabelle De Singleton, 38. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Margaret. Он умер после 1397 г.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Nicholas de Singleton nació en 1312, en Kirkham, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Gilbert de Singleton, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Isabelle De Singleton, tenía 38 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Margaret. Murió después de 1397.
